title: Dev Osei
compiled_truth:
Dev Osei is a Partner at [Foundry Partners](companies/foundry-partners), where he leads the firm's developer tools and infrastructure practice. Before joining Foundry, he spent five years as an operator — first at a YC-backed observability startup that got acqui-hired, then as head of developer relations at a mid-stage platform company. That operator background shows up in how he evaluates deals: Dev is less interested in pure technical novelty and more focused on go-to-market wedge, distribution moats, and whether a founding team can actually sell to developers without alienating them.

He's known internally at Foundry as a sharp pattern matcher. His thesis on developer tools is straightforward — he believes the next wave of infrastructure companies will win by collapsing multi-step workflows into single primitives, and that the real defensibility comes from embedding early in CI/CD pipelines and data paths where switching costs compound over time. This framing is why he got interested in Anchor Codec's approach to deterministic data infrastructure; he saw parallels with other portoflio companies that succeeded by reducing pipeline complexity.

Dev sits on the boards of [Vellum Systems](companies/vellum-systems) and [Keel Networks](companies/keel-networks), and has observer seats at [Nexus Relay](companies/nexus-relay) and [Forge Build](companies/forge-build). Across these companies, he tends to push hard on GTM strategy — sometimes to the point of friction with deeply technical founders who want to stay in build mode longer. That said, most of his portfolio founders describe him as genuinely helpful once they're ready to start selling. He has a strong referral network among enterprise DevOps buyers, particularly in fintech and healthcare verticals.

Osei is based in Atlanta but travels to SF roughly once a month. He's active on the conference circuit — he spoke at InfraConf 2027 on "Why Developer Tools Companies Die in the Middle" and has been on several podcasts about infrastructure investing. He tends to write long, slightly rambling deal memos that bury the strongest insights three pages in. His partners joke about this but also acknowledge that his conviction calls have the best hit rate on the team.

Within the Anchor Codec context, Dev has been a consistent advocate for accelerating enterprise sales motions and pushing the team to formalize their channel strategy earlier than they might otherwise.
timeline:
- **2026-03-14** | Dev Osei first introduced to Anchor Codec founding team through a warm intro from a [Vellum Systems](companies/vellum-systems) board member.
- **2026-05-22** | Initial diligence call with Anchor Codec; Dev focused almost entirely on GTM positioning and competitive landscape.
- **2026-08-10** | [Foundry Partners](companies/foundry-partners) participates in Anchor Codec's seed round; Dev takes an observer seat.
- **2026-11-03** | Hosts an informal dinner in Atlanta connecting Anchor Codec founders with two enterprise DevOps buyers from his network.
- **2027-02-18** | Joins [Keel Networks](companies/keel-networks) board after leading their Series A alongside another Foundry partner.
- **2027-06-09** | Speaks at InfraConf 2027 on developer tools go-to-market — references Anchor Codec's single-pass architecture as an example of "collapsing the stack."
- **2027-09-25** | Pushes Anchor Codec team to hire a dedicated head of sales, leading to a mildly contentious board discussion about timing.
- **2027-12-11** | Facilitates intro between Anchor Codec and [Forge Build](companies/forge-build) to explore potential integration partnership.
- **2028-03-04** | Circulates internal memo at Foundry arguing for pro-rata in Anchor Codec's Series A, citing pipeline embeddability as key moat.
- **2028-06-19** | Co-hosts a closed-door roundtable with [Nexus Relay](companies/nexus-relay) and Anchor Codec teams on convergent infrastructure patterns in regulated industries.

_facts.name: Dev Osei
_facts.title: Partner, Foundry Partners
_facts.background: Partner at Foundry Partners; focuses on developer tools investments
